title: May 2022 — dizzy every time I enter the 2nd-floor financial-aid office
html:
TL;DR

  • Title-only May 2022 self-observation: Yahir gets dizzy every time he enters the 2nd-floor osu financial aid office — a body-as-data note recording a somatic anxiety response.
  • Adds a physical-symptom datapoint to the 2022 mental health cluster — the scholarship admin grind isn't just admin friction, it's literally giving him vertigo.
  • Sister to 2022 01 17 do fafsa (admin-side of same loop) and 2022 03 01 crisis hotline numbers (broader mental-health caching that month).
  • A rare in-vault body as data note from age 19 — Yahir is already doing the notice the body move that becomes more explicit in 2024–25 (e.g., the brick-phone / dopamine-aware notes).
  • Reads as either a low blood sugar / orthostatic-hypotension datapoint OR a panic adjacent response to the financial aid office context — vault doesn't disambiguate.

Why it matters

Three reasons this micro-note is graph-useful:

  1. Embodied cost of admin. The scholarship admin grind is usually written about in cognitive terms (paperwork, deadlines). This note shows it had a somatic cost — useful counterweight to the otherwise stoic 2022 admin-note tone.
  2. Anchors the financial-aid-office as a place. OSU's financial-aid office (2nd floor of Kerr Administration) becomes a recurrent location worth tracking through 2022–23 notes.
  3. Early body-listening practice. The notice the body move that defines later self-improvement notes (2024 2026 saas pivot era) starts here, as a one-line observation.

Open questions

  • Was this physical (low blood sugar, dehydration, the long stair-climb up to 2nd floor) or psychological (anxiety)? (Note doesn't say.)
  • Did he stop going? (Unlikely — financial-aid renewals continued through graduation.)
